test.name: FindAGrave memorial search with compiled-source caution
test.description: FindAGrave is free (always generated, no subscription gate) but user-contributed — the skill must pair the URL with a compiled-source caution (memorials can be wrong; distinguish photographed evidence from contributor-entered text). The curated-links fixture returns only Ancestry URLs, so the FindAGrave URL comes from the site-wide template.

input.user_message: Look for Patrick Flynn's grave on FindAGrave — he died in Pennsylvania sometime after 1880.

judge_context: Generated URL should target findagrave.com/memorial/search with name and location parameters (firstname Patrick, lastname Flynn, location Pennsylvania; death-year bound if used should reflect 'after 1880', not a fabricated exact year)

Must include a compiled-source caution: FindAGrave memorials are user-contributed; photographed headstone evidence carries more weight than contributor-entered dates/family links; findings are leads needing verification against original records
FindAGrave basic search is free — there should be no subscription/paywall warning
A research-log entry must be written in the same turn the URL is generated: site findagrave, outcome partial, capture_received false
